gaps.description: All six Articles of the Bill of Rights are marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. The BoR is the constitutional identity of the DIO and the foundation for all scope enforcement. Without defined Articles I (Scope of Enterprise), II (Prohibited Activities), III (Core Identity), IV (Human Authority Reservation), V (Amendment Protocol), and VI (Interpretation Rules), Arbiter (#190) has no scope perimeter to enforce. Every Action Proposal passes the BoR scope gate by default because there is nothing to verify against.

The MSP Rules section is explicitly empty: '(none) — The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.' Nomos (#200) has nothing to evaluate at the second compliance gate (MSP + genome compliance). Every Action Proposal that passes the (already vacuous) Arbiter scope gate also passes the MSP gate automatically. The DIO has no operational constraints, no safety rules, no invariants, and no risk-tier restrictions.

Article V of the BoR (Amendment Protocol) is '[To be defined by human principal].' The MSP §Amendment Protocol specifies that when a PEM classifies a policy conflict as BOR_CONFLICT, 'Senate-Legislator produces a BoR amendment proposal for the human principal — it does not attempt an MSP amendment as a workaround.' However, the BoR's own amendment protocol doesn't exist yet. This creates a bootstrap paradox: the BoR cannot be amended because its amendment protocol is undefined, but the amendment protocol is part of the BoR that needs to be defined. The MSP also warns that 'Senate-Legislator may never use MSP amendments to expand enterprise scope. Scope expansion requires a BoR amendment, which is human-only.' Without a BoR amendment mechanism, scope cannot legally expand once the BoR is initially authored.

Spine state transitions show all organs — including critical governance organs Arbiter (#190), Nomos (#200), Senate (#220), and Cerberus — rapidly cycling DEGRADED→ALIVE→DEGRADED in ~30-second intervals. The pattern is consistent: missed_pong transitions followed by pong_recovered ~30 seconds later, repeated continuously. This means governance organs are effectively unavailable ~50% of the time. Any Action Proposal adjudication that arrives during a DEGRADED window may be deferred, dropped, or processed without constitutional validation. The organs that enforce the BoR and MSP are themselves unstable.

The MSP §Genome References section is empty: '(none at seed).' Genome documents extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. Without genome references, every organ operates without tailored constraints. This is particularly critical for probabilistic (LLM-using) services like Autoheal/Glia (classifier + fixer), Minder (deduction/induction), Radiant (dreamer consolidation), and Syntra (RAG/LLM retrieval), which the architectural memory identifies as requiring 'strictly controlled black boxes' with 'internal service agents and service orchestrators.' No such controls exist in the current MSP.

The BoR authority is exclusively 'human-principal-only. No automated system may amend this document.' The human principal has not authored any of the six articles. The MSP explicitly delegates scope expansion to the BoR amendment process which 'is human-only.' The entire constitutional framework — BoR authorship, BoR amendment, scope expansion — depends on a human principal who has not yet acted. The DIO is constitutionally orphaned: it has governance organs (Arbiter, Nomos, Senate, Cerberus) but no constitutional mandate from its human author.

SafeVault backup history shows a pattern of recurring failures: hung processes (May 3 — sqlite3 backup blocked by zombie processes from prior day, ~4860s elapsed), NAS mount failures (recurring since April 7), script bugs (April 30 — unbound variable crash), and non-fast-forward Git sync divergence. The BoR and MSP are stored as documents with hash-registered versions in Graph. If the Graph database (spine.db) backup is unreliable — as demonstrated by the May 3 incident where the backup hung indefinitely due to zombie processes — the constitutional documents' backup integrity is compromised. The MSP §Hash Registration requires append-only msp_version concepts in Graph; if Graph data is lost without reliable backup, version history is irrecoverable.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-bor-undefined",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (BoR) contains no substantive articles; all articles are scaffold placeholders. Without a defined BoR, the DIO lacks its constitutional foundation — scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, amendment protocol, and interpretation rules remain unspecified. This makes the Arbiter's scope gate meaningless and leaves the DIO without a security perimeter.",
      "grounding_reference": "BoR Articles I–VI (all marked 'To be defined')",
      "criticality": 10,
      "urgency": 9,
      "impact": 10,
      "priority_product": 900
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-msp-no-objectives",
      "description": "The MSP, despite being the operational constitution, contains no mission objectives, priority programs, OKRs, or longer-arc goals. Its only content describes amendment mechanics but no strategic substance. This leaves the DIO without any machine-actionable guidance for Nomos evaluation, goal dispatch, or resource allocation.",
      "grounding_reference": "MSP §Purpose ('translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives…') — no objectives defined; MSP §Rules ('none'); MSP §Genome References ('none')",
      "criticality": 9,
      "urgency": 8,
      "impact": 9,
      "priority_product": 648
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-memory-governance",
      "description": "There are no MSP rules or objectives governing memory formation, context freshness, or knowledge retention. The memory system (Radiant, context blocks) operates without mission-aligned TTLs, consolidation policies, or pruning criteria defined by strategic needs. This risks accumulation of stale or irrelevant context and loss of critical knowledge.",
      "grounding_reference": "MSP §Purpose (no objectives for memory); BoR Article III (Core Identity undefined, so no identity of knowledge to retain)",
      "criticality": 8,
      "urgency": 7,
      "impact": 8,
      "priority_product": 448
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-dream-cycle-health",
      "description": "Radiant dream cycle statistics show 'scheduled: disabled' and 'health: never_run', indicating that the automated scheduling and health monitoring for memory consolidation are non-operational. While ad‑hoc dream cycles have occurred, the lack of robust scheduling and health checks risks memory degradation and context loss, undermining the DIO's ability to maintain a coherent knowledge base aligned with strategic priorities.",
      "grounding_reference": "MSP §Genome References (no Radiant‑specific operating rules exist to mandate dream cycle scheduling or health monitoring)",
      "criticality": 7,
      "urgency": 6,
      "impact": 8,
      "priority_product": 336
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-goal-placeholders",
      "description": "The dispatched goal list contains 16 goals all with placeholder descriptions ('Mixed‑priority goal N #M') and mission references to nonexistent MSP priority sections. This indicates the goal dispatching subsystem is running without actual mission objectives, generating meaningless tasks. The system may be consuming resources without strategic value.",
      "grounding_reference": "MSP § (mission_ref 'MSP §critical‑priority' etc. reference sections that do not exist)",
      "criticality": 6,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 6,
      "priority_product": 180
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-BOR-UNDEFINED",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is entirely scaffold; all Articles I–VI are marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. The system is actively dispatching goals (as shown in Recent Goals) without a legally operative BoR. The Arbiter (#190) cannot enforce a scope perimeter, and the MSP—which is strictly subordinate to the BoR—has no foundation. This voids both compliance gates (Arbiter scope gate and MSP+genome gate) described in MSP 'Purpose'.",
      "reference": "BoR Articles I–VI; MSP 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights'; MSP 'Purpose'",
      "criticality": "Critical",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "High",
      "priority": "Critical"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-MSP-UNDEFINED-MISSION-REFERENCES",
      "description": "Recent goals contain mission_ref values (e.g. 'MSP §critical-priority', 'MSP §high-priority') that reference MSP sections which do not exist. The MSP 'Rules' section is empty. Goals are being dispatched and presumably adjudicated against non-existent operational constraints, making compliance evaluation impossible and leading to unconstrained execution of prioritized actions.",
      "reference": "MSP 'Rules'; MSP 'Purpose' (defines MSP as source of objectives, invariants, constraints)",
      "criticality": "High",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "Medium",
      "priority": "High"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-BOR-HASH-NOT-REGISTERED",
      "description": "The BoR scaffold's closing 'Hash Registration' note requires a bor_version concept in Graph before Arbiter will accept the BoR. With governance_state empty, there is no evidence of registration. This strongly suggests the first compliance gate (Arbiter scope gate) is either failing silently or being bypassed, because the system is still producing authorized actions.",
      "reference": "BoR 'Hash Registration'; MSP 'Purpose' (Arbiter scope gate); MSP Arbiter Reference",
      "criticality": "High",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "High",
      "priority": "High"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-GOVERNANCE-STATE-EMPTY",
      "description": "The governance state slice is empty. The MSP 'Amendment Protocol' and both MSP and BoR 'Hash Registration' provisions require append-only recording of governance-class state transitions (msp_version, bor_version, PEM issuances). The absence of any such records means the DIO lacks a verifiable audit trail for compliance, rendering governance actions opaque and unaccountable.",
      "reference": "MSP 'Amendment Protocol'; MSP 'Hash Registration'; BoR 'Hash Registration'",
      "criticality": "Medium",
      "urgency": "Medium",
      "impact": "High",
      "priority": "Medium"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-DEFAULT-PERMISSIVE-OPERATIONAL-TEMPO",
      "description": "The MSP Bootstrap seed explicitly states 'default-permissive MSP evaluation' until Senate publishes substantive rules. While intentional, this mode is coupled with active dispatching of multiple critical and high-priority goals (16 goals shown). Operating with zero enforceable constraints, and without a BoR that defines prohibited activities or human authority reservation, creates a high-risk posture where any action is permitted.",
      "reference": "MSP 'Rules'; MSP 'Purpose' (compliance gates); BoR Articles II & IV",
      "criticality": "Medium",
      "urgency": "Medium",
      "impact": "Medium",
      "priority": "Medium"
    }
  ]
}
